vocabulary.definition: A personal name (typically a last name for best accuracy) submitted to the API for nationality prediction. Full names are accepted and automatically parsed. Diacritics are handled via automatic fallback matching.
An ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 two-letter country code identifying a predicted country of nationality (e.g. 'VN' for Vietnam, 'US' for United States).

A decimal confidence score between 0 and 1 indicating how likely the queried name belongs to the associated country. The sum of all probabilities in a result set may be less than 1 because only the top five results are returned.
The number of data points (people with that name) in the underlying dataset used to compute the nationality prediction.
A single API request containing up to 10 names submitted using the array query parameter syntax `name[]=`. Each name counts individually toward the monthly usage quota.
An API authentication key passed as a query parameter. Required for paid plans and to track usage. The same key is shared across the Demografix suite (Genderize, Agify, Nationalize).
Accent marks and special characters used in names (e.g. é, ü, ñ). The API automatically retries with diacritics removed if an initial match fails.
The company behind Nationalize.io, Genderize.io, and Agify.io — three complementary name-inference APIs sharing a common API key.
The predicted country of cultural or ethnic origin associated with a given name, represented as an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code. Distinct from citizenship or legal nationality.
The default unauthenticated (or low-volume authenticated) access level providing up to 2,500 name predictions per month at no cost.
